prompt: Clean educational technical infographic for a reverse osmosis lesson, white background, crisp modern engineering style, legible labels, flat vector-like composition rendered as PNG. Main diagram compares natural osmosis and reverse osmosis side by side. Left panel block by block: block 1 is a left water chamber labeled low solute or fresh water; block 2 is a central semipermeable membrane shown as a vertical barrier; block 3 is a right chamber labeled high solute or salt water; block 4 is a blue arrow crossing the membrane from low solute to high solute labeled natural osmosis. Right panel block by block: block 1 is a high-pressure feed side with saline water and a pressure piston icon pushing inward; block 2 is a semipermeable membrane; block 3 is a clean-water permeate side; block 4 is a blue arrow crossing the membrane toward the clean-water side labeled reverse osmosis; block 5 is retained contaminants on the feed side shown as ions and particles unable to pass. Bottom strip block by block: block 1 is a mini dead-end filtration sketch with water going straight into a filter and particles accumulating on the surface; block 2 is a mini cross-flow filtration sketch with feed water moving parallel to the membrane, permeate passing through, and concentrate continuing to the drain.

Multi-panel educational infographic about water quality metrics for reverse osmosis, clean lab style, flat technical illustration, white background, blue and green accents. Panel 1 block by block: a hand holding a digital TDS meter immersed in a glass of water, digital display visible. Panel 2 block by block: a simple conductivity-to-TDS conversion chart with axis labels and a conversion factor note 0.5 to 0.7. Panel 3 block by block: SDI test concept with a membrane disc becoming darker as particulates clog it over time. Panel 4 block by block: LSI scaling risk shown as calcium scale depositing onto a membrane surface. Panel 5 block by block: chlorine and chloramine symbols feeding into a carbon pre-filter block, then a TFC membrane block with a red warning icon if chlorine reaches the membrane. Make all labels large and readable.

Clean block diagram of a standard 5-stage domestic reverse osmosis system, technical training style, white background, high readability, all flow paths clearly colored. Diagram block by block from left to right: block 1 is the feed water inlet and shutoff valve; block 2 is a sediment filter housing; block 3 is a carbon pre-filter housing; block 4 is a second carbon filter housing; block 5 is the RO membrane housing. From the membrane block, upper branch block by block: a permeate outlet, a check valve, a storage tank, and a faucet. Lower branch block by block: concentrate outlet, flow restrictor, and drain line. Add a small side block for the automatic shut-off valve connected logically between feed and tank pressure. Use arrows and labels for feed, permeate, and concentrate.

Comparison diagram of reverse osmosis pre-filters, educational product-cutaway style, clean white background. Layout has three main vertical blocks. Left block by block: a melt-blown polypropylene sediment filter cutaway with suspended dirt, rust, and silt particles trapped through depth filtration pathways. Middle block by block: a granular activated carbon cartridge with irregular carbon granules and dissolved chlorine or organics adsorbing onto surfaces. Right block by block: a carbon block cartridge with dense uniform media, smaller pores, and improved fine filtration. Bottom callout strip block by block: icon for particle capture, icon for chlorine removal, and highlighted note for chloramines requiring catalytic carbon.

Exploded technical diagram of a spiral-wound reverse osmosis membrane, clean engineering illustration, white background, flat vector-like rendering as PNG. Center block by block: a permeate collection tube in the middle. Around it, layered blocks by block: permeate carrier layer, polyamide thin-film active layer, polysulfone support layer, polyester backing, and feed spacer mesh. Add arrows showing feed water entering along the membrane surface, purified permeate moving inward to the center tube, and concentrate continuing outward to the reject outlet. Include a small inset block by block for concentration polarization: membrane wall, salt boundary layer building up, and turbulence from cross-flow breaking the layer apart.

Process diagram for post-filtration and remineralization after reverse osmosis, educational technical style, white background. Diagram block by block from left to right: block 1 is RO permeate labeled low mineral and slightly acidic; block 2 is a coconut-shell carbon polishing filter removing taste and odor; block 3 is a remineralization cartridge containing calcite and magnesium oxide media; block 4 is a glass of finished water labeled with improved taste and higher pH. Add small chemistry callouts for carbon dioxide absorption and buffering restoration.

Cutaway educational diagram of a hydropneumatic reverse osmosis storage tank, white background, clear labels, modern engineering illustration. Show three tanks across the image as a progression. Left tank block by block: empty tank, small water volume, large air chamber, label 7 to 8 PSI pre-charge. Middle tank block by block: partially filled tank, moderate compressed air chamber, rising pressure arrows. Right tank block by block: nearly full tank, compressed air chamber, high internal pressure, note that the automatic shut-off valve closes at roughly two-thirds of feed pressure. Add simple Boyle's law annotation.

Split comparison diagram of reverse osmosis pump options, clean technical infographic, white background, blue accents for water and orange accents for energy or pressure. Left side block by block: feed water line entering an electric booster pump, then a pressure switch, then a membrane housing receiving boosted pressure, with labels 80 to 100 PSI and higher rejection. Right side block by block: brine line driving a permeate pump mechanism, internal piston or diaphragm transfer, permeate line being pushed into the tank, and membrane isolated from tank backpressure. Show arrows for energy flow and water flow clearly.

Educational infographic about RO waste water and efficiency, white background, modern flat style, clean labels. Top section block by block: feed water entering a membrane block and splitting into a smaller permeate stream and a larger concentrate stream, labeled with a simple recovery ratio such as 1 part product water to 3 or 4 parts brine. Bottom section has three comparison blocks. Left block: correctly sized flow restrictor balancing pressure and flushing. Middle block: restrictor too small causing fouling and scaling on the membrane. Right block: restrictor too large causing excess brine to drain and poor pressure buildup.

Three-column comparison visual for choosing a reverse osmosis system, clean product comparison infographic on white background. Column 1 block by block: standard undersink tanked system with filter housings, membrane, tank, and faucet. Column 2 block by block: compact tankless direct-flow unit with integrated booster pump and proprietary cartridge form factor. Column 3 block by block: simplified comparison icons for countertop and whole-house options. Under each column include small badges for output, noise, electricity requirement, maintenance, and cost. Add a small highlighted note for tankless systems showing TDS creep after idle periods.

Workshop flat-lay style instructional illustration of the tools and materials needed for RO installation, arranged neatly on a white background, clean technical product style. Individual labeled objects block by block: tubing cutter, push-to-connect fittings, blue collet clips, adjustable wrench, drill, titanium step bit, PTFE tape, pressure gauge, and coiled RO tubing. Each object should have a short callout describing its purpose.

Under-sink plumbing diagram for installing a domestic reverse osmosis system, realistic cabinet layout but simplified for teaching, white background, highly legible colored tubing. Diagram block by block: top left is the cold-water angle stop with an RO feed adapter valve; center is the mounted pre-filters and membrane assembly; bottom center is the storage tank; top right is the faucet on the sink deck, with an optional air-gap faucet variant indicated; bottom right is the drain saddle mounted above the P-trap on the vertical tailpiece. Draw each tube path clearly in different colors for feed, permeate, brine, and air-gap drain line, with labels.

Step-by-step startup sequence graphic for a new reverse osmosis system, white background, clean infographic style, four sequential blocks with arrows between them. Block 1: new filters and dry membrane installed. Block 2: system filling the storage tank for the first time over 2 to 3 hours. Block 3: faucet opened and dark or foamy first water being discarded. Block 4: repeating the flush cycle again. Add a warning callout that first water should not be consumed.

Safety-focused reverse osmosis leak testing diagram, white background, instructional style with green checks and red warnings. Main block: complete RO system at static pressure with highlighted likely leak points at housings, push fittings, tubing connections, and check valve area. Side block 1: close-up of a push fitting with the blue collet clip correctly installed. Side block 2: a cabinet floor leak detector shutoff valve using an expanding sponge mechanism. Make leak points easy to understand.

Instructional figure about handling an RO membrane correctly, technical clean white-background illustration. Left block by block: membrane cartridge with the brine seal clearly highlighted on one end. Middle block by block: membrane housing and correct insertion direction arrow showing feed entering the brine-seal end. Right block by block: close-up of permeate tube O-rings with a small amount of food-grade silicone grease being applied. Add a red warning symbol with petroleum jelly crossed out.

Monitoring diagram for inline TDS measurement in an RO system, white background, crisp technical style. Block 1: feed water line with inline TDS probe 1 installed. Block 2: RO membrane housing. Block 3: permeate line with inline TDS probe 2 installed. Block 4: digital monitor displaying feed ppm, permeate ppm, and rejection rate. Add a small inset block by block showing a thermistor and automatic temperature compensation correcting conductivity readings.

Maintenance dashboard infographic for reverse osmosis service intervals, white background, clean and modern, easy to scan. Main section block by block: a timeline or calendar bars for sediment filter, carbon filters, membrane, and sanitization intervals. Side inset block 1: delta-pressure monitoring with a pressure gauge before the pre-filters and another before the membrane. Side inset block 2: biological loading shown as bacteria growth risk in an old wet carbon filter over time.

Symptom-to-cause diagnostic flowchart for common reverse osmosis faults, clean educational infographic on white background. Start row block by block: symptom boxes for continuous draining, rapid cycling, and low permeate flow. Branches block by block lead to likely causes such as failed check valve, waterlogged tank, bad automatic shut-off valve diaphragm, membrane fouling, scaling, or cold feed water. Use simple icons for drain flow, clicking noise, and weak faucet output.

Stepwise sanitization protocol diagram for a reverse osmosis system, white background, technical hygiene infographic. Sequential blocks by block: remove all filters and membrane; add bleach or hydrogen peroxide to the first housing; run system until disinfectant reaches the faucet; let the system sit pressurized for contact time; flush thoroughly and reinstall new cartridges. Include a caution panel reminding the viewer to rinse fully before use.

Hardware architecture block diagram for an IoT-enabled reverse osmosis monitor, white background, modern technical schematic style. Diagram block by block: feed-line TDS sensor; permeate-line TDS sensor; Hall-effect flow sensor; DS18B20 temperature sensor; central ESP32 microcontroller; Wi-Fi or MQTT cloud or broker connection; Home Assistant dashboard or smartphone notification panel. Show signal arrows and label each data stream clearly.

Comparison infographic for advanced reverse osmosis upgrades, clean white background, educational engineering style. Left block by block: UV-C sterilizer as the final stage, showing the lamp, quartz sleeve, and water path. Right block by block: auto-flush solenoid valve on the brine line, connected to a timer relay, with a strong flush arrow sweeping across the membrane surface. Add small benefit badges for microbiological safety and reduced scaling.

Large-scale whole-house reverse osmosis system block diagram, white background, highly legible industrial schematic style. Diagram block by block: raw water feed; pretreatment skid; high-pressure pump; parallel 4x40 membrane vessels; atmospheric permeate storage tank; repressurization delivery pump; household plumbing distribution. Add a side branch for concentrate reject going to drain and a warning callout that downstream piping should be PEX or CPVC.

Reuse pathways diagram for reverse osmosis concentrate, clean white background, practical sustainability infographic style. Center block: RO system with permeate and brine outputs. From the brine output branch block by block to three destinations: hot-water line through a zero-waste pump, toilet cistern refill, and irrigation or rain barrel storage. Add a side warning note about algae growth in clear containers and mention that brine is only moderately higher in TDS than feed water.

Service business model diagram for residential reverse osmosis maintenance, white background, polished startup-style infographic. Diagram block by block: multiple installed customer RO units; telemetry stream from each unit; central monitoring dashboard; technician dispatch based on alerts only; recurring monthly subscription revenue. Add small icons for lease, annual filter service, membrane replacement, and leak-repair coverage.

Final recap course-map infographic for the osmosis course, clean educational summary design on a white background. Main flow block by block from left to right: Foundations, System Design, Installation, Monitoring and Maintenance, Advanced and Applied. Inside each large block include several smaller topic labels from the course such as osmosis principles, water testing, membranes, tanks, installation plumbing, leak testing, TDS monitoring, troubleshooting, IoT, brine reuse, and business models. Connect the large blocks with progression arrows.
